reasoning: The possibility of Harvard University losing its 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status by July 1, 2025 (in slightly over two months) is virtually nonexistent. Although the Trump administration has strongly threatened this and reportedly requested the IRS to take action against Harvard's tax status as of mid-April 2025, standard IRS revocation procedures involve a lengthy process of audits, notices, and administrative appeals that universally span years, not weeks. Furthermore, a 1998 federal law prohibits the president from directing the IRS to target specific taxpayers. Even if the administration attempts an expedited and unlawful process, Harvard—the wealthiest university in the U.S. with formidable legal resources—would instantly seek and almost certainly obtain preliminary injunctions or temporary restraining orders in federal court, delaying any official loss of status until well past July 2025. Given the legal hurdles, procedural requirements, and inevitable extreme litigation, an incredibly short timeline of just over two months makes this outcome effectively impossible.
